articles.title: APPLE IS PLANNING SOMETHING BIG FOR ITS 50TH BIRTHDAY
articles.summary: Apple will celebrate its 50th birthday on April 1. CEO Tim Cook has promised "some celebration" during an internal company meeting. The details remain unclear, and it's uncertain whether this will be an employee-only event or include public festivities. Apple may release special anniversary accessories, such as themed Apple Watch bands or iPhone cases, though the company could also launch something bigger for this milestone.

articles.title: EU SAYS TIKTOK'S 'ADDICTIVE DESIGN' LIKELY VIOLATES THE LAW, WILL NEED TO CHANGE
articles.summary: The European Commission issued preliminary findings that TikTok's addictive features, like infinite scroll, autoplay, and personalized algorithms, likely violate EU Digital Services Act regulations designed to protect users, especially minors. Regulators claim TikTok failed to assess how these design choices harm users and ignored warning signs of compulsive behavior among children using the app late at night. If confirmed, TikTok could face fines up to 6% of its global revenue and must make design changes.

articles.title: STUDIO DISPLAY 2 COMING SOON WITH AT LEAST FOUR BIG CHANGES
articles.summary: Apple is reportedly preparing to release the Studio Display 2 soon. It will have a similar design but notable internal upgrades, including a higher refresh rate (90Hz or 120Hz), an A19 chip, mini-LED backlighting, and HDR support. The update is expected in the first half of the year, though pricing and connectivity details remain unknown.

articles.title: SOME THOUGHTS ABOUT TOOL DESIGN AND AI
articles.summary: Current AI tools poorly align with good tool design principles: they exhibit high latency with multi-second waits, require text descriptions rather than hands-on control, and impose usage costs or limits. Emerging tools like real-time video manipulation models offer a contrast by allowing immediate interaction, being open-source, and enabling users to shape outputs directly. AI tooling should evolve toward faster, more directly manipulable systems that reduce the gap between user intent and execution, particularly as local models improve.

articles.title: HOW GENERATIVE AI IS REDEFINING BRAND IDENTITY SYSTEMS
articles.summary: Generative AI is transforming brand identity from rigid, fixed assets into flexible systems that adapt across contexts. Designers are shifting from crafting individual assets to becoming system architects who define constraints, behaviors, and brand logic. Successful implementation requires establishing a clear core brand DNA, defining what stays fixed and what is flexible, building governance early, and addressing ethical considerations around bias and inclusivity.

articles.title: UX DESIGNERS DON'T NEED TO BE DATA SCIENTISTS — BUT THEY MUST CHALLENGE DATA
articles.summary: UX designers today must understand data and metrics to connect their design work to business impact, beyond just creating intuitive user experiences. They must be able to interpret analytics, question data findings, and understand when quantitative metrics don't align with qualitative user research. The role has evolved from focusing solely on usability to requiring collaboration with data teams and the ability to challenge data when it doesn't tell the complete user story.

articles.title: BEST GLOBAL BRANDS 2025
articles.summary: The world's most extensive longitudinal study on the role that brands play in driving revenue and creating market value.

articles.title: BUILD MOTION-FIRST MULTI-PAGE WEBSITES
articles.summary: Fimo helps teams build motion-first multi-page websites with AI-powered workflows, collaborative editing, and automated publishing.

articles.title: RUN DESIGN PROJECTS LIKE A STUDIO
articles.summary: Honter is a platform that helps designers run design projects like a professional studio with organized workspaces for branding, web, and ongoing tasks.

articles.title: HOW TYPOGRAPHY HIERARCHY BOOSTS UI USABILITY
articles.summary: Typography hierarchy organizes text by importance through size, weight, color, spacing, and position, enabling users to scan interfaces efficiently. An effective hierarchy reduces cognitive load, improves scannability, supports faster decision-making, and enhances accessibility. Common mistakes include using too many font sizes, overusing emphasis, inconsistent styling, poor contrast, and inadequate spacing.

articles.title: TWO REASONS HELPED APPLE HIT RECORD IPHONE SALES, AND THEY'RE BOTH ORANGE
articles.summary: Apple reported record iPhone sales last quarter driven largely by a surge in China, where the new iPhone 17 Pro models' eye-catching Cosmic Orange color—resembling Hermès orange and emphasizing status and luxury—played a key role in boosting demand.

articles.title: HOW TO RUN A LOGO DESIGN CONTEST – A SIMPLE GUIDE FOR STARTUPS
NEW SUPER BOWL LOGO, SAME OLD CONSPIRACY THEORY
articles.summary: The newly revealed Super Bowl LXI logo, which features orange and light blue on a purple background, has reignited fan conspiracy theories that its colors predict the finalist teams and winner, despite the theory being wrong in previous years.

articles.title: THIS APPLE EASTER EGG HAS BEEN HIDING IN PLAIN SIGHT
articles.summary: Apple fans discovered that the paper emoji secretly contains the opening line of the iconic 1997 “Think Different” campaign.
